engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 607 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 607 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level HDi diesel for early 607 models; reliable but prone to injector wear at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Injector seal deterioration leading to fuel leaks (80k+ miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey use
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10ATED Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Facelift update brought higher output and refined injection; improved economy but retained common HDi vulnerabilities.
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure causing overheating (70k+ miles)
DPF regeneration issues on urban-driven cars
Swirl flap motor failure on intake manifold
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10TED4 Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The most common 607 diesel; robust block but infamous for timing belt pulley bolt shearing causing catastrophic top-end failure.
compatibleVariants: 2.2 HDi 133, 2.2 HDi Executive, 2.2 HDi SE, 2.2 HDi Sport
commonFailures: Timing belt pulley bolt shearing leading to bent valves (60k-100k miles)
Injector failure causing rough running and smoke
Overheating from coolant sensor or fan relay faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW12TED4 Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: High-output facelift variant with variable geometry turbo; more power but increased DPF and turbo wear on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: 2.2 HDi 170, 2.2 HDi Executive, 2.2 HDi Sport, 2.2 HDi GT
commonFailures: DPF blockage requiring costly regeneration or replacement (80k+ miles)
Turbo oil seal leaks causing blue smoke and oil consumption
EGR valve carbon buildup reducing performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW12BTED4 Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: Peugeot's flagship HDi V6 offering executive refinement; complex twin-turbo setup prone to expensive turbo and injector failures.
compatibleVariants: 2.7 HDi V6 Executive, 2.7 HDi V6 GT, 2.7 HDi V6 Sport
commonFailures: Twin turbo failure due to oil starvation or actuator faults (70k+ miles)
Piezo injector failure causing misfires and hard starting
Complex electrical sensor faults triggering limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for DT17TED4 Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth, refined V6 petrol flagship; reliable mechanically but electrical gremlins and coil pack failures common on older examples.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 V6 207 (pre-facelift), 3.0 V6 211 (facelift), 3.0 V6 Executive, 3.0 V6 GT
commonFailures: Ignition coil pack failure causing misfires and rough idle (60k+ miles)
Camshaft position sensor faults triggering engine management light
Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for ES9J4S Peugeot 607 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range petrol option offering balance of performance and economy; generally reliable but ignition system components need attention.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure causing cylinder misfires (70k+ miles)
Spark plug wear leading to poor fuel economy
Thermostat housing leaks causing coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW12J4 Peugeot 607 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 607 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 607's HDi diesels (especially the 2.2 DW12) and V6 units dominate UK replacement enquiries due to known failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your 607.

problems.group: Peugeot 607 Timing Belt Pulley Bolt Failure
problems.h4: Peugeot 607 Timing Belt Pulley Bolt Failure - Why DW12 2.2 HDi Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.rootCause: The timing belt tensioner pulley bolt can shear without warning, causing the belt to jump and resulting in bent valves and piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace original bolt with upgraded specification before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, re-machine surface, new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; labour-intensive with risk of hidden damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt unit with upgraded components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; eliminates risk of residual damage

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 607 worth £1,500-£4,000, a preventative bolt upgrade is excellent value if your DW12 engine is still healthy. If the belt has jumped, replacement with a rebuilt unit is usually more cost-effective than extensive head repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW12 engine prices for your Peugeot 607

problems.group: Peugeot 607 2.7 HDi V6 Turbo & Injector Failure
problems.h4: Peugeot 607 2.7 HDi V6 Turbo & Injector Failure - Complex Diesel Challenges
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 607 2.7 HDi V6 Executive, GT, Sport (2004-2010, DT17TED4)
problems.rootCause: Twin turbochargers suffer oil starvation or actuator faults; piezo injectors are sensitive to fuel quality and wear, causing misfires and hard starting.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty actuator or vacuum lines; clean vanes if accessible
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if turbo internals are sound; not viable if bearings worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and code new piezo injectors; recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbos are healthy; requires specialist diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt V6 with upgraded turbos and injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage V6s; eliminates multiple failure points at once

problems.recommendation: The DT17 V6 is complex and expensive to repair piecemeal. If your 607 has over 90k miles and shows turbo or injector symptoms, a rebuilt engine often delivers better long-term reliability than sequential repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 607 2.7 HDi V6 replacement engines

problems.h4: Peugeot 607 DPF Blockage & EGR Issues - Facelift Diesel Headaches
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; EGR valves clog with carbon, reducing airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and chemically clean DPF; clean or blank EGR valve
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires regular motorway runs to maintain

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned DPF unit; reset ECU adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is otherwise healthy; does not address underlying usage patterns

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with updated emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if DPF failure coincides with other wear items like injectors or turbo

problems.recommendation: If your facelift HDi 607 is used mainly for short trips, a DPF delete (where legal) or conversion to a non-DPF setup may be more practical than repeated cleaning. For high-mileage engines with multiple issues, replacement offers better value.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 607 HDi engine prices with DPF solutions

problems.group: Peugeot 607 3.0 V6 Petrol Electrical & Ignition Faults
problems.h4: Peugeot 607 3.0 V6 Petrol Electrical & Ignition Faults
problems.rootCause: Ignition coil packs degrade with age; camshaft and crankshaft sensors fail, causing misfires, rough idle, and engine management warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty coils and sensors; clear fault codes and test drive
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated electrical faults; coils should be replaced as a set

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all coils, plugs, leads and relevant sensors; full diagnostic reset
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine compression is good; addresses multiple potential failure points

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt V6 with upgraded ignition components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine shows mechanical wear alongside electrical faults

problems.recommendation: Electrical faults on the ES9 V6 are often misdiagnosed as mechanical issues. Start with a thorough diagnostic check; if compression is healthy, coil and sensor replacement is usually sufficient.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Peugeot 607 3.0 V6 engine options

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 607
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 607's executive engineering demands specialist knowledge for reliable repairs. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand DW10, DW12, DT17 and ES9 engines inside out. Every rebuilt unit includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices from trusted suppliers and keep your 607 on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 607 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 607 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 607 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 607 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 607 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi was the entry diesel for early 607 models, offering strong economy and adequate performance for the executive saloon. Facelift models received a higher-output variant with refined injection mapping.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 607 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 607 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L HDi became the 607's most popular diesel, balancing performance and refinement. However, the DW12 family is notorious for timing belt pulley bolt shearing causing catastrophic top-end failure.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 607 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 607 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The flagship 2.7L HDi V6 offered executive refinement and strong performance but introduced complex twin-turbo and piezo injector systems prone to expensive failures on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 607 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 607 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.2L 16v petrol provided a smoother, quieter alternative to diesel for early 607 models, with generally reliable mechanicals but ignition system components requiring attention as mileage increases.

items.title: Peugeot 607 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L V6 petrol was the 607's performance flagship, delivering refined power and smooth delivery. Mechanically robust but electrical gremlins and coil pack failures are common on older examples. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot 607 needs? items.descriptor: Diesel HDi engines dominated 607 sales in the UK, with 2.0L, 2.2L and 2.7L V6 options covering economy to executive performance across pre-facelift and facelift generations.
families: DW10 - 2.0L HDi (109/136hp), entry diesel with injector and EGR vulnerabilities
DW12 - 2.2L HDi (133/170hp), most common but timing belt pulley bolt risk
DT17 - 2.7L HDi V6 (204hp), complex twin-turbo with high repair costs
foundIn: Facelift saloon (2004-2010) - DW10TED4, DW12BTED4, DT17TED4, ES9J4S revised
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy on all HDi variants
DW12 timing belt pulley bolt failures between 60k-100k miles
DPF and EGR issues on facelift models used for short journeys
High UK replacement demand on 2.2 HDi 133/170 variants

items.title: Peugeot 607 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol options provided refinement and simplicity, with the 2.2L 16v for early models and the smooth 3.0L V6 flagship across both generations.
items.descriptor: Petrol options provided refinement and simplicity, with the 2.2L 16v for early models and the smooth 3.0L V6 flagship across both generations.
families: EW12 - 2.2L 16v (158/163hp), reliable but ignition coils need attention
ES9 - 3.0L V6 (207/211hp), refined flagship with electrical sensor quirks
knownFor: Smooth, quiet operation especially on the V6
Ignition coil pack failures causing misfires on both petrol units
Camshaft/crankshaft sensor faults triggering engine management light
Lower replacement demand than diesel but higher unit value for V6

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 607 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 607's 2000-2010 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Launch generation introduced Peugeot's executive saloon with DW10, DW12 and ES9 engines establishing the core lineup.
keyChanges: Peugeot 607 launched with 2.0 HDi 109, 2.2 HDi 133 and 3.0 V6 207 petrol
2.2 petrol EW12 option added for non-diesel buyers
Early DW12 engines used original pulley bolt design later linked to failures
knownFor: Robust mechanical design but early DW12 pulley bolt vulnerability
ES9 V6 praised for refinement but electrical sensors can fail
Strong residual values for well-maintained pre-facelift examples
Timing belt intervals critical - missed changes cause catastrophic failure
years.cta: Get quotes for pre-facelift Peugeot 607 replacement engines

keyChanges: New DW10TED4 (2.0 HDi 136) and DW12BTED4 (2.2 HDi 170) with higher power
Flagship DT17TED4 2.7 HDi V6 added for executive buyers
ES9J4S revised to 211hp with updated engine management
DPF fitted to facelift diesels meeting Euro 4 emissions
knownFor: DPF blockage on facelift HDi models used for short journeys
DT17 V6 turbo and injector complexity driving high replacement costs
DW12BTED4 retains pulley bolt risk despite power increase
Facelift electronics more prone to sensor faults than pre-facelift

faq.h2: Peugeot 607 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot 607 engine?
items.answer: Peugeot 607 engine replacement typically costs £1,100-£2,800 for rebuilt HDi units and £2,000-£4,500 for V6 engines, supply only. Prices vary by engine code, condition and supplier warranty terms.
keyPoints: DW12 2.2 HDi rebuilt units from £1,100-£2,800 supply only
DT17 2.7 HDi V6 commands £2,200-£4,500 due to complexity
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 depending on garage location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 607 engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Peugeot 607?
items.answer: Replacement makes sense if your 607 is in good condition elsewhere and valued above £2,000. Rebuilt engines cost less than dealer repairs and extend life significantly.
keyPoints: Compare vehicle value against rebuild cost before deciding
Rebuilt DW12 engines often cheaper than cylinder head repairs
V6 owners may find replacement more economical than turbo/injector fixes
EngineMarket quotes help you compare options transparently
items.cta: Get a Peugeot 607 engine replacement quote today

items.question: What engines fit the Peugeot 607?
items.answer: Only engines with matching codes fit: DW10/DW12/DT17 for diesel, ES9/EW12 for petrol. Pre-facelift and facelift engines are not interchangeable without modification.
keyPoints: DW12TED4 (pre-facelift) and DW12BTED4 (facelift) differ in turbo and ECU
DT17TED4 V6 is facelift-only and not compatible with earlier models
Always verify engine code via DVLA or physical inspection before ordering
EngineMarket specialists confirm compatibility before dispatch

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 607 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days. Full supply & fit services require 2-4 days including removal, fitting and road testing.
keyPoints: Used engines may dispatch faster if in stock with supplier
Rebuilt units require build time; confirm lead time when quoting
Supply & fit includes ancillary checks and fluid replacement
Nationwide delivery available from most UK specialists
items.cta: Check Peugeot 607 engine availability and lead times

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 607 replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier and engine condition type.
keyPoints: Rebuilt and reconditioned units typically carry 12-month coverage
Used engines may have shorter warranties; confirm before purchase
Warranty covers mechanical failure, not misuse or installation error
Keep all documentation for warranty claims

items.question: Can I replace a Peugeot 607 engine myself?
items.answer: DIY replacement is not recommended due to the 607's complex electronics, timing procedures and specialist tool requirements. Professional fitting ensures warranty validity.
keyPoints: DW12 timing belt procedure requires precise tensioning tools
DT17 V6 needs specialist diagnostic equipment for injector coding
Incorrect installation voids supplier warranty and risks damage
Supply & fit quotes often provide better value than DIY risks
items.cta: Find Peugeot 607 engine supply & fit specialists

items.question: How quickly can a Peugeot 607 engine be delivered?
items.answer: Most suppliers offer next-day or 48-hour delivery for in-stock engines. Rebuilt units may require 3-5 days build time before dispatch.
keyPoints: Used engines often available for immediate dispatch
Rebuilt units built to order; confirm lead time when requesting quotes
Nationwide courier delivery included in most supply-only quotes
Urgent requirements should be flagged when submitting enquiry
items.cta: Get fast Peugeot 607 engine delivery quotes

items.question: Should I choose used or rebuilt for my Peugeot 607 engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines offer better longevity and warranty protection, especially for DW12 units with known pulley bolt issues. Used engines suit tight budgets on lower-value cars.
keyPoints: Rebuilt DW12 engines include upgraded pulley bolts addressing known failure
Used engines cheaper but carry unknown mileage and wear history
V6 engines benefit more from rebuild due to complexity and repair costs
EngineMarket lets you compare both options side-by-side
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Peugeot 607 engine prices

items.question: Where is the engine code located on a Peugeot 607?
items.answer: Engine codes are stamped on the block near the gearbox flange or on a sticker in the engine bay. DVLA lookup via registration is the fastest verification method.
keyPoints: DW12 codes often visible on timing belt cover edge
DT17 V6 code stamped on cylinder head near injector rail
Registration lookup via EngineMarket instantly confirms correct code
Always double-check code before ordering to avoid compatibility issues
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 607 engine code instantly

items.question: What are the most common Peugeot 607 engine problems?
items.answer: DW12 2.2 HDi timing belt pulley bolt failure, DT17 V6 turbo/injector faults, and facelift DPF/EGR issues dominate replacement enquiries across the 607 range.
keyPoints: DW12 pulley bolt shearing causes bent valves if belt jumps
DT17 twin turbos prone to oil starvation and actuator faults
Facelift HDi models suffer DPF blockage on short-journey use
ES9 V6 electrical sensor faults common but often misdiagnosed
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 607 problem-engine replacement
